LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANTS JOHN HICKS, NEILL AMICK, AND ZACH TAYLOR'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (OF FRANK YUEN, ET AL) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 7TH - 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY AND CONTRIBUTION. THE DOCTRINE OF INDEMNITY ONLY APPLIES AMONG DEFENDANTS WHO ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS. 5 WITKIN SUMMARY 10TH (2005) TORTS SECTION 115, P. 212. A RIGHT TO CONTRIBUTION EXISTS BY STATUTE IN CALIFORNIA "WHERE A MONEY JUDGMENT HAS BEEN RENDERED JOINTLY AGAINST ONE OR MORE DEFENDANTS IN A TORT ACTION..." CCP SECTION 875(A). NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. DEMURRER OVERRULED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Piper (362.6286) for cross defendants and moving parties; B. Douglas Robbins of O'Leary Wood & Robbins LLP (247.7900 x204) for cross complainant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANTS JOHN HICKS, NEILL AMICK, AND ZACH TAYLOR'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (OF FRANK YUEN, ET AL) is sustained without leave to amend as to 7th - 12th causes of action for indemnity and contribution. The doctrine of indemnity only applies among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiffs. 5 Witkin Summary 10th (2005) Torts section 115, p. 212. A right to contribution exists by statute in California "where a money judgment has been rendered jointly against one or more defendants in a tort action..." CCP section 875(a). No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overruled in all other respects, 10 days leave to answer. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

PLAINTIFF JOHN HICKS, NEILL AMICK, ZACH TAYLOR's Motion To Compel Production of Document and for money sanctions is granted.
